title: ebraxis muscipula
intro: Ebraxis muscipula, also known as the Venus flytrap, is a carnivorous plant species native to the bogs of North and South Carolina in the United States. The medicinal properties of Ebraxis muscipula lie in its ability to stimulate digestive enzymes and aid in the breakdown of proteins, making it useful in traditional medicine for treating various ailments. The health benefits of consuming the plant include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Ebraxis muscipula can be made by drying the leaves and roots, then infusing them in hot water or oil to create a tincture or salve.
